full_prompt: Design a magnetically coupled transcutaneous drug delivery reservoir for an implantable medical device project. The device is a subcutaneously implanted 316L stainless steel reservoir (internal volume 5.0 mL, external OD 25 mm, length 35 mm, wall 0.5 mm) with a titanium septum cap and a magnetically actuated micro-pump valve driven by an external handheld N52 neodymium magnet held over the skin. The external magnet applies a vertical force of 0.5 N through skin thickness up to 5 mm, actuating the implanted pump to dispense up to 100 uL per actuation. The reservoir must maintain sterility for up to 3 months of implantation per ASTM F2097. Pass/fail criteria: (R1) max von Mises stress in the 316L reservoir under normal physiological tissue pressure of 5 kPa plus external manual palpation force of 20 N must not exceed 115 MPa (316L yield 170 / SF 1.5), (R2) magnetic coupling must deliver at least 0.3 N at the pump valve through 5 mm skin using N52 magnet of 20 mm diameter x 10 mm thickness (Br 1.45 T), analytically checked via dipole approximation, (R3) septum leak rate at 10 kPa internal pressure must not exceed 0.1 uL per hour (ASTM F2097), (R4) device mass must not exceed 20 g to meet implantable comfort target, and (R5) titanium housing + septum must survive 50 ethylene-oxide sterilization cycles at 55 C with no dimensional change exceeding 0.05 mm. Thermal analysis only to confirm sterilization cycle thermal envelope. Vibration, fluid CFD, and radiation analyses are not required; drug reservoir leak is a closed-form pressurized-vessel calculation. Deliver a STEP AP242 assembly with reservoir, septum, pump valve, and magnet holder as sub-bodies plus a verification report listing reservoir stress, magnetic force at pump, septum leak rate, device mass, and post-sterilization dimensional deviation.
short_prompt:
Design a magnetically coupled transcutaneous drug-delivery reservoir for a subcutaneous implant. The device is a 316L stainless steel cylindrical reservoir (internal volume 5.0 mL, external ~25 mm OD x 35 mm long, 0.5 mm wall) with a Grade 2 titanium + silicone septum cap and a magnetically actuated micro-pump valve. Actuation is via a handheld external N52 neodymium magnet (20 mm dia x 10 mm, Br 1.45 T) held over the skin (up to 5 mm thick), dispensing up to 100 uL per actuation.

Design to ASTM F2097 (implantable port leak) and assume 3-month implant sterility. The reservoir sits in soft tissue with the septum on the skin side.

Load cases.
- LC1 palpation + tissue: 20 N manual palpation plus 5 kPa physiological tissue pressure.
- LC2 magnetic coupling: external N52 magnet pulls the valve through 5 mm skin (dipole approximation).
- LC3 septum leak test: 10 kPa internal pressure.
- LC4 ETO sterilization: 50 cycles at 55 C (dimensional stability).

Total device mass is an implantable-comfort constraint.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: External N52 magnetic coupling force through skin is closed-form magnetic dipole / Gilbert model, not FEA.

prompt.title: Magnetically actuated transcutaneous drug reservoir
prompt.brief: Design a 5 mL 316L/Ti implantable drug reservoir (25 x 35 mm) with magnetically actuated pump valve, meeting palpation stress, magnetic coupling force, septum leak, mass, and ETO sterilization dimensional stability.

load_cases.name: magnetic_coupling
load_cases.external_magnet: N52, 20 mm dia x 10 mm, Br 1.45 T

boundary_conditions.reservoir: surrounded by soft tissue; septum exposed to skin side

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: axisymmetric solid for reservoir, 3D solid for valve

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Implant is static; no harmonic loads.
exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Drug flow is pressure-driven; device scope limited to reservoir.

out_of_scope_but_real_world_needed: FDA 510(k) predicate search and IP disclosure
